The classification of Campylocentrum hispidulum provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other fern species.

intro: Campylocentrum hispidulum is a species of epiphytic orchid native to tropical forests in Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Campylocentrum hispidulum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Campylocentrum hispidulum is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and teas, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
